entries.title: Historic Vermont floods
entries.description: Floods of November 3, 1927 triggered reports of strange bodies in swollen rivers and renewed old hill folklore.

entries.title: Wilmarth-Akeley correspondence begins
entries.description: By spring 1928, Wilmarth's public debate draws Akeley's letters and private exchange about Vermont hill phenomena.

entries.title: Townshend and Brattleboro geography
entries.description: Dark Mountain, Round Hill, and surrounding roads are remote, wooded, and difficult to monitor at night.

entries.title: Occult circles whisper of Alhazred fragments
entries.description: Private reports mention passages attributed to the Necronomicon regarding entities that traverse the outer gulfs and barter forbidden knowledge.
